steps.title: Isolate the square
steps.content:
Start with the equation:
$$x^2-\frac{16}{49}=0$$
Move the constant to the other side:
$$x^2=\frac{16}{49}$$

steps.title: Count the real roots
steps.content: A positive value for $x^2$ gives two real square roots: $$x=\pm\sqrt{\frac{16}{49}}=\pm\frac{4}{7}$$ There are exactly two distinct real solutions. The answer is C.
